title: glehnia littoralis
intro: Glehnia littoralis, also known as sweetroot or licorice root chrysanthemum, is a perennial plant species native to East Asia, particularly in China, Japan, and Korea. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Glehnia littoralis include reducing fever, relieving cough and asthma symptoms, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory infections. Glehnia littoralis can be prepared as a decoction or tea for its medicinal uses, with some herbal practitioners also using it in combination with other herbs to enhance its effects.
